name: Policy Resilience Framing Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reframe digital identity as critical infrastructure → Systemic: 25% faster scaling through regulatory alignment with eIDAS2 → Strategic: Controls supplier concentration risk vs. compliance complexity.
options: Anchor proposals in national security terminology to bypass privacy-focused objections
Leverage EU interoperability mandates as a compliance-driven pressure point
Introduce blockchain-based audit trails for procurement transparency
review: Controls Supplier Concentration vs. Compliance Complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider geopolitical shifts in platform vendor policies.

name: Technical Demonstration Architecture
consequences: Immediate: Build FIDO2/WebAuthn demonstrators → Systemic: 15% reduction in platform-specific code dependencies → Strategic: Controls technical risk vs. adoption friction.
options: Use GrapheneOS as a baseline for privacy-respecting mobile authentication
Develop browser-based authentication with WebAuthn and OpenID Connect
Integrate hardware tokens via NFC and open-source firmware
review: Controls Technical Risk vs. Adoption Friction. Weakness: The options underweight the complexity of cross-platform certification under NSIS.

name: Procurement Language Influence
consequences: Immediate: Draft platform-neutral procurement clauses → Systemic: 20% increase in alternative vendor consideration → Strategic: Controls regulatory capture vs. competitive innovation.
options: Require multi-platform authentication support in all public-sector contracts
Institute mandatory third-party audit requirements for identity systems
Create a 'digital sovereignty' scoring metric for vendor evaluations
review: Controls Regulatory Capture vs. Competitive Innovation. Weakness: The options neglect the legal ambiguities of enforcing EU-wide standards domestically.

name: Stakeholder Coalition Dynamics
consequences: Immediate: Formalize civil-society partnerships → Systemic: 30% stronger advocacy network coverage → Strategic: Controls political resistance vs. institutional legitimacy.
options: Establish a cross-sector advisory board with public-private stakeholders
Create a 'Digital Identity Resilience Fund' for alternative vendors
Deploy academic research partnerships to generate policy-adjacent publications
review: Controls Political Resistance vs. Institutional Legitimacy. Weakness: The options fail to account for conflicting interests within the digital policy committee.

name: Fallback Authentication Path Design
consequences: Immediate: Develop hardware-token workflows → Systemic: 18% improvement in contingency planning maturity → Strategic: Controls user convenience vs. system robustness.
options: Implement SIM-based authentication with open-standard SIM card interfaces
Create a decentralized certificate authority model using open-source frameworks
Adopt a quantum-resistant cryptographic layer for fallback systems
review: Controls User Convenience vs. System Robustness. Weakness: The options underweight the operational costs of maintaining dual authentication infrastructure.
